title: banksia borealis subsp. elatior
intro: Banksia borealis subsp. elatior, also known as the Giant Banksia, is a subspecies of the Banksia borealis, a flowering plant native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support respiratory health due to its high levels of sesquiterpene compounds. The leaves and bark of the Banksia borealis subsp. elatior have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory issues, and its extracts have shown potential in supporting the health of the lungs and airways. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to promote respiratory well-being and reduce inflammation.
